_comment: P37 jurisdictional-overlay sources (FL first; NC added in P37-P4). Load: python3 tools/source_currency.py seed-sources canonical-sources/jurisdiction-sources-seed.json. Government GIS boundary services + statutes that feed the optional advisory overlays. Public-domain federal + open state/county data (cache freely, per-layer metadata); statutes are public-domain edicts. Model building-code text (ICC/FBC) is copyrighted -> cache the adopted-edition FACT only. ArcGIS editingInfo.lastEditDate / OGC ETag is the token-free freshness signal.
name: FEMA National Flood Hazard Layer - Flood Hazard Zones (ArcGIS REST)
extraction_hint: point flood-zone query on layer 28 (FLD_ZONE, SFHA_TF, STATIC_BFE) in inSR=4326; layer 28 has no editingInfo, so poll the LOMRs layer 1 for change
_license: US federal work, public domain (17 USC 105); not the legally authoritative FIRM

name: FEMA NFHL - Letters of Map Revision (change signal)
extraction_hint: LOMR effective/case dates; the regulatory-change signal for NFHL since layer 28 lacks editingInfo

name: South Florida Water Management District open-data GIS (ArcGIS Hub)
extraction_hint: district/basin boundaries + ERP layers; ArcGIS Hub item modified date / REST lastEditDate
_license: FL public agency open data; per-item license, generally open/public-domain

name: City of Miami historic districts (ArcGIS open data)
extraction_hint: local historic-district boundary polygons (design-review trigger); Hub item modified / REST lastEditDate
_license: municipal public records / open data; per-item license

name: Florida Statute 553.899 - Milestone structural inspections (SB 4D)
extraction_hint: condo/co-op >=3 stories, milestone by year building turns 30 then every 10y; the year in the URL path is the codification-change signal

name: Florida Statute 553.842 - Statewide product approval (HVHZ authority)
extraction_hint: statewide product evaluation/approval; underpins HVHZ product control (Miami-Dade NOA / TAS)

name: Southeast Florida Regional Climate Compact - Unified Sea Level Rise Projection
extraction_hint: the regional SLR projection edition (2019 current, ~5yr revision); the value informs local freeboard ordinances; watch the edition year
_license: regional compact report; cite the projection facts + edition, do not mirror the report

name: NC Mountain Ridge Protection Act ridgelines (NC OneMap ArcGIS)
extraction_hint: statewide protected-ridge lines (topo-map digitized, SCREENING ONLY); static unless the MRPA is amended; the FeatureServer editingInfo/item modified is the freshness signal
_license: NC OneMap / NCGS open, public; no warranty (accuracy); attribute to NCGS

name: Buncombe County GIS - protected ridges + zoning overlays (ArcGIS)
extraction_hint: layer 7 Protected Ridges, layer 20 County Zoning Overlay, Slope Stability Index Map; steep-slope % itself is DEM-derived (no slope-percent layer)
_license: county public records / open GIS; standard accuracy disclaimer; attribute to Buncombe County GIS

name: Rutherford County GIS - reference layers (parcels/flood only)
extraction_hint: parcels + flood zones only; Rutherford has NO countywide zoning and NO steep-slope ordinance, so slope must be DEM-derived
_license: county public records / open GIS; standard accuracy disclaimer

name: NC Mountain Ridge Protection Act (Ch. 113A, Article 14)
extraction_hint: protected ridge = elevation >= 3,000 ft AND >= 500 ft above the adjacent valley; tall building > 40 ft; counties/cities opt in

name: Buncombe County zoning ordinance (Ch. 78 Art. VI, steep-slope/ridge overlays)
extraction_hint: Sec. 78-644 Steep Slope/High Elevation (above 2,500 ft, slope <= 35%); Sec. 78-645 Protected Ridge (above 3,000 ft); geotech trigger at existing grade >= 36% or mapped hazard
_license: county ordinance = public-domain law; the .gov PDF is fetchable, Municode hosting is not

name: Asheville local historic districts + downtown design review (ArcGIS open data)
extraction_hint: local historic overlay + downtown design review district (Asheville Code Sec. 7-9-2); boundary polygons via the city ArcGIS hub

name: NC water-supply watersheds + river basins (NC OneMap / DEQ)
extraction_hint: NC DEQ water-supply-watershed (WS) protection overlays + major river basins (French Broad, Broad); ArcGIS REST / Hub modified date
